title: Electricity generation from renewables
subtitle: Measured in [terawatt-hours](#dod:watt-hours). Renewable sources include solar, wind, hydropower, bioenergy, geothermal, wave, and tidal.
caption:
Electricity generation from renewables, 2025
Measured in terawatt-hours. Renewable sources include solar, wind, hydropower, bioenergy, geothermal, wave, and tidal.

This choropleth map displays global electricity generation from renewable sources in 2025, measured in terawatt-hours. The color scale ranges from pale green for the lowest generation levels to dark blue for the highest, with specific bands marking 0, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1,000, and 2,000 TWh. China and the United States occupy the darkest blue band, indicating generation exceeding 1,000 TWh, standing as the two largest producers visible on the map. Brazil and Canada appear in a medium-dark blue band, falling between 200 and 500 TWh. India and Russia are shaded in a lighter blue, placing them in the 100 to 200 TWh range. Across Europe, countries such as Germany, France, Spain, and the United Kingdom are visible in the 50 to 100 TWh band, while nations like Sweden and Norway show slightly higher output in the 100 to 200 TWh category. Australia, Mexico, and Argentina sit in the 20 to 50 TWh band, represented by a light green-blue shade. Much of Africa and the Middle East appears in the palest green shades, indicating generation below 20 TWh, with many countries falling under the 10 TWh threshold. Greenland, Western Sahara, and several smaller island nations are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data is available. The distribution shows a concentration of high-volume renewable electricity generation in East Asia and North America, with moderate levels across South America and parts of Europe, and lower levels across most of the African continent and Central Asia.
